title: The One With Rachels Inadvertent Kiss

jokes.text: Oh, boy. That guy's underwear sucks! What? Well, I got this pair marked 'XS'. Let me tell you, there was no room for excess anything in there.
jokes.setup: Joey commenting on Ralph Lauren underwear quality
jokes.punchline: Reveals he bought XS size with no room for 'excess anything' - euphemism for his anatomy
jokes.explanation: Classic Joey humor - physical comedy meets sexual innuendo with his characteristic obliviousness. Well-structured with good misdirection from 'sucks' to the real punchline.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: I bought Ralph Lauren underwear. XS. There's no room for excess anything.

jokes.text: Oh, my God! What did I ever do to you? Did I squeeze too hard? Let's just say I'm glad I'm not Chandler.
jokes.setup: Monica practicing handshakes, Ross tries and reacts in pain
jokes.punchline: Monica's response implies she regularly hurts Chandler with her grip strength
jokes.explanation: Good character-based humor playing on Monica's competitive nature and physical strength, with implication about her relationship with Chandler.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: What? Chandler loves it!

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Monica, you're breaking my hand!

jokes.text: Is he doing his 'shark attack' bit? No. Oh, wait! There he goes.
jokes.setup: Joey watching Ross through window across buildings
jokes.punchline: Ross apparently has a recurring 'shark attack' routine he does alone
jokes.explanation: Absurd visual comedy that's perfectly economical. The idea that Ross has a routine 'shark attack bit' he does alone is wonderfully specific and weird.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: What is he doing?

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: I don't know, but I'm scared and aroused.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Is that... is he doing the shark?

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Oh my God, he's doing the shark!

jokes.text: Very funny, Ross. Very lifelike and funny.
jokes.setup: Ross doing his shark attack bit
jokes.punchline: Chandler's deadpan commentary treating it like legitimate entertainment
jokes.explanation: Classic Chandler sarcasm with perfect delivery. 'Very lifelike' is an unexpectedly specific critique of a shark attack impression.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Duuuh duuuh, duuuh duuuh...

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Could that BE any more shark?

jokes.text: How... you... doing?
jokes.setup: Joey trying to flirt with woman across buildings
jokes.punchline: His signature pickup line delivered with dramatic pauses
jokes.explanation: Iconic Joey catchphrase delivered in perfect context. While not original within the show's universe, it's perfectly integrated and highly quotable.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: How you doin'?

jokes.text: No, no! I'm not with her. That's just Monica.
jokes.setup: Someone comments on the 'pretty' girl with Chandler
jokes.punchline: Chandler's horrified correction that it's Monica, his girlfriend
jokes.explanation: Decent relationship humor about not wanting to be seen as 'with' your girlfriend, though somewhat mean-spirited toward Monica.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: That's not a pretty girl, that's Monica! She's my girlfriend!

jokes.text: I counted. You're not supposed to live here! Oh, man!
jokes.setup: Joey confidently enters what he thinks is the hot girl's apartment
jokes.punchline: He miscounted and ended up at Ross's place instead
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection payoff. Joey's confidence followed by immediate deflation works well, and 'not supposed to live here' is a funny way to phrase it.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Hey baby, it's me, Joey Tribbiani. I'm here to sweep you off your feet.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Joey, what are you doing in my apartment?

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Wait, what? This isn't... Oh man, I miscounted the doors again.

jokes.text: No, Joe, I think he's just watching TV.
jokes.setup: Joey thinks Ross is doing a 'watching TV bit'
jokes.punchline: Chandler's dry observation that Ross is actually just watching TV
jokes.explanation: Perfect Chandler deadpan, deflating Joey's assumption that everything Ross does is performance art. Great economy.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: No, he's actually just watching TV.

jokes.text: It's so unfair that our date has to get cut short just because some guy shot at a store clerk.
jokes.setup: Phoebe complaining about Gary having to leave for police work
jokes.punchline: Treating violent crime as minor inconvenience to her dating life
jokes.explanation: Excellent Phoebe humor - her self-centered worldview makes violent crime about her inconvenience. Dark but perfectly in character.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: I can't believe Gary has to leave for police work. We were supposed to have dinner tonight!

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: I mean, there's a violent crime happening, but what about us? This is so inconsiderate.

jokes.text: Suspect has just emerged naked from the sewer. All right, you go.
jokes.setup: Phoebe wanting to come along on police call
jokes.punchline: Radio describes increasingly bizarre crime scene, making her change her mind
jokes.explanation: Good escalation from generic crime to absurd naked sewer situation. Phoebe's quick retreat is the perfect reaction beat.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: I want to go on a police call with you!

dialogue.speaker: Police Radio
dialogue.line: We have a 10-31 in progress at the corner of 5th and Main. Suspect is armed and dangerous.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Oh my God, okay, maybe not.

dialogue.speaker: Police Radio
dialogue.line: Wait, we're getting reports of a second suspect. And he's... he's covered in what appears to be peanut butter.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Peanut butter? That's... that's weird.

dialogue.speaker: Police Radio
dialogue.line: And there's a llama. I don't know how the llama got here, but it's very angry.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Okay, definitely not going.

jokes.text: He's like this little puppy dog, you know? But like a really tough one who shoots bad guys.
jokes.setup: Phoebe describing why she misses Gary
jokes.punchline: Comparing cop to puppy but qualifying with 'shoots bad guys'
jokes.explanation: Great juxtaposition between cute/innocent and violent/dangerous. Very Phoebe to reconcile these contradictions so matter-of-factly.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: I miss Gary. He was like a puppy, you know? Loyal, eager to please... except he shoots bad guys.

jokes.text: Okay, you know where you are better than I do.
jokes.setup: Monica insisting she and Chandler are still in honeymoon phase
jokes.punchline: Rachel's diplomatic but skeptical concession
jokes.explanation: Good diplomatic humor. Rachel's polite skepticism is relatable - when someone insists too hard, you just agree to end it.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Oh, totally. You guys are still totally in the honeymoon phase.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: I mean, sure, you bicker constantly and he sleeps in the guest room, but... honeymoon phase!

jokes.text: God, I woke the beast.
jokes.setup: Monica becoming defensive about her relationship
jokes.punchline: Rachel realizing she triggered Monica's competitive side
jokes.explanation: Perfect economical metaphor. 'Woke the beast' captures both Monica's competitive nature and Rachel's regret at triggering it.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: What? I was just saying my relationship is going really well.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Oh, YOUR relationship is going well? Mine is PERFECT. It's not just well, it's perfect.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Monica, I didn't say—

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: And we have sex like five times a week. FIVE TIMES.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Okay, I see what's happening here.

jokes.text: Here's your broom back. You are so cute!
jokes.setup: Monica trying to prove she and Chandler are passionate
jokes.punchline: Awkward domestic moment treated as sexy - returning a broom
jokes.explanation: Brilliant subversion - most unromantic interaction (returning cleaning supplies) treated as passionate moment. The mundane made absurd.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: See? We're passionate!

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Monica, you're returning a broom.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: A broom we bought together!

jokes.text: Did you say you wanted to have sex with his wife then fall off your chair?
jokes.setup: Rachel saying she did something embarrassing at interview
jokes.punchline: Chandler references his own legendary interview disaster
jokes.explanation: Great callback that serves the story. Sets up expectation that nothing could be worse than Chandler's legendary failure.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: I can't believe I did that in the interview. I'm so embarrassed.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Oh please, you want to talk about interview disasters? I once told my interviewer that his tie looked like a dead animal. Turns out it was.

jokes.text: Well, yeah, if you've got enough life jackets.
jokes.setup: Rachel recounting her successful joke during interview
jokes.punchline: Her boat-related quip about life jackets
jokes.explanation: Weak dad joke. Without context of the original boat comment, it falls flat. Generic workplace humor.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: So I was being interviewed, right? And they asked me about my biggest accomplishment. I told them, 'Well, I've never needed a life jacket because I've always managed to stay afloat in this industry.' They loved it!

jokes.text: You could have tried not kissing him. Thanks, Chandler.
jokes.setup: Rachel explaining she didn't know what else to do but kiss
jokes.punchline: Chandler's obvious solution delivered deadpan, Rachel's sarcastic thanks
jokes.explanation: Classic Chandler logic meets Rachel sarcasm. The obviousness of the solution makes it funnier.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Well, hey, if you ever find yourself in that situation again...

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Oh, thank you! That really helps!

jokes.text: Let's bring back the girl who kisses everybody!
jokes.setup: Rachel worried about why she got second interview
jokes.punchline: Her paranoid interpretation of employer's motivation
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Rachel's anxiety. The specificity of 'the girl who kisses everybody' makes it funnier than generic worry.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Why would they call me back for a second interview? Do you think they know something? Like, what if they Googled me and found that embarrassing photo from Monica's wedding?

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Or maybe they're just being nice because they feel bad for me. Oh my God, what if I'm their charity case?

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Actually, you know what? They probably just want to see if I'll cry again like I did at the first interview.

jokes.text: She's back! Hot girl's back! Well, I'm not totally back yet. But I appreciate it.
jokes.setup: Joey excited about 'hot girl' returning
jokes.punchline: Rachel thinks he's talking about her return, responds graciously
jokes.explanation: Nice misdirection with Rachel's innocent misunderstanding. Her gracious acceptance of the compliment she thinks she received.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Oh man, she's coming back!

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Aw, Joey, that's so sweet. I knew you'd miss me.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Miss you? I'm talking about the hot girl from the coffee shop!

jokes.text: I lived with you for a while. Then I found this place.
jokes.setup: Joey asks if Chandler moved
jokes.punchline: Chandler's matter-of-fact explanation of the obvious
jokes.explanation: Solid Chandler deadpan. The obviousness of the explanation contrasts with Joey's confusion.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Did you move?

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: No, I'm just here visiting my stuff.

jokes.text: Hey, little girl. Is your mommy or sister or babysitter by any chance, a hot girl?
jokes.setup: Joey desperately searching for the hot girl
jokes.punchline: Inappropriately questioning a child about 'hot' adults
jokes.explanation: Perfectly cringey Joey moment. His desperation leads to inappropriate conversation with child, but stays just innocent enough.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Have you seen a really hot girl around here? She's about yay high, blonde hair?

dialogue.speaker: Child
dialogue.line: Um, no...

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Well, if you do, can you tell her Joey Tribbiani is looking for her? And hey, while I'm asking - you ever see any hot moms dropping you off at school?

jokes.text: I do not want this job that bad. Good day, sir.
jokes.setup: Rachel misreading situation as sexual harassment
jokes.punchline: Her dramatic exit with formal 'Good day, sir'
jokes.explanation: Good escalation with Rachel's righteous indignation. 'Good day, sir' is perfectly formal and quotable for dramatic exits.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: That is sexual harassment and I will not stand for it!

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Good day, sir.

jokes.text: You got ink on your lip.
jokes.setup: Ross seemingly knows Rachel kissed someone
jokes.punchline: Simple explanation - she has ink on her lip
jokes.explanation: Elegant misdirection. Builds mystery of how Ross knows, then deflates with simple visual explanation. Very economical.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: You have ink on your lip.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: What?

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Right there. You have ink on your lip.

jokes.text: Then why did you ask us if we wanted to go? Because I just wanted to know what you were doing so you wouldn't walk in on me and Chandler while we were doing it all night.
jokes.setup: Monica claiming they have movie plans when they don't
jokes.punchline: Reveals she was just trying to coordinate so friends won't interrupt their all-night sex
jokes.explanation: Monica's competitive oversharing escalates awkwardly. Her strategic thinking about scheduling sex is very Monica.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: We have movie plans.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: We do?

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Yeah, all night.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Oh! Oh!

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: I just didn't want anyone showing up and interrupting.

jokes.text: Just to impress them, you want us to have sex over and over and I'm saying no to this, why? Get your coat!
jokes.setup: Chandler objecting to Monica's competitive sex plan
jokes.punchline: Realizes he's arguing against having lots of sex, immediately changes position
jokes.explanation: Excellent structure with Chandler's logical reversal. Perfect male logic - objecting to sex on principle until realizing it's still sex.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Wait, wait, wait. Monica, that's not a good idea. We shouldn't be competitive about this.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Why not? It'll be fun!

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Because it'll just turn into... I mean, we'll be... Hold on. Did I just argue against having more sex?

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: You know what? Bring it on. Let's do this. I'm in.

jokes.text: I know. You're the guy who wouldn't chip in for the handyman. Never mind.
jokes.setup: Ross trying to introduce himself to building neighbor
jokes.punchline: She knows him as the cheap guy who wouldn't contribute to building maintenance
jokes.explanation: Good character consistency - Ross's cheapness precedes him. The immediate dismissal after recognition is brutal and funny.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Hi, I'm Ross Geller. I live in 4G.

dialogue.speaker: Neighbor
dialogue.line: Oh yeah, I know who you are. You're the cheap guy who wouldn't chip in for the building maintenance.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: That was one time!

jokes.text: No, you're the best. No, you're the best. I am the best.
jokes.setup: Monica and Chandler complimenting each other after sex
jokes.punchline: Chandler breaks the cycle by accepting he's the best
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of post-coital politeness. Chandler's matter-of-fact acceptance of superiority breaks the Canadian politeness loop.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: You're amazing.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: No, you're amazing.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: No, you are.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: No, you are.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: No, you are.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Okay, fine. I'm amazing.

jokes.text: I don't know, but I can see through your sheet.
jokes.setup: Joey asking if that's the girl who waved
jokes.punchline: Chandler's attention drawn to Joey's inadequate covering
jokes.explanation: Visual comedy about Joey's poor towel situation. Economical way to add awkwardness to the scene.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Is that the girl who waved?

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Where? Oh my God, you're right! She's still waving!

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Well, wave back!

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: I can't wave back, I'm not wearing any pants!

jokes.text: She lives in some hot-girl parallel universe or something.
jokes.setup: Joey frustrated at inability to find the girl's apartment
jokes.punchline: His theory that hot girls exist in alternate dimension
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Joey logic - when faced with practical problem, invents elaborate metaphysical explanation. 'Hot-girl parallel universe' is perfectly specific and quotable.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: How is it possible that I cannot find this girl's apartment? I've been walking around for like an hour.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: You know what? I think hot girls live in an alternate dimension. That's the only explanation.

jokes.text: I almost lost another girl because of counting!
jokes.setup: Monica explaining Joey's counting error
jokes.punchline: Joey's revelation that counting has cost him multiple women
jokes.explanation: Excellent Joey character work. The idea that basic counting has repeatedly sabotaged his romantic life is absurd and perfectly in character.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Joey, you can't count like that. You've lost at least three women because of your counting.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Wait, what? Three women? Who?

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Well, there was that girl from the coffee shop, and the one from the gym, and—

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Oh, come on! That's not my fault. That's not about counting!

jokes.text: When I thought you wanted sex in exchange for this job, I said no. That shows integrity.
jokes.setup: Rachel trying to salvage her interview
jokes.punchline: Bragging about rejecting sexual harassment as job qualification
jokes.explanation: Good cringe humor - Rachel presenting basic human decency as impressive job qualification. Shows her desperation.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Well, I have to say, I'm very proud of myself. I've never sexually harassed anyone at work.

dialogue.speaker: Interviewer
dialogue.line: That's... that's good?

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: I mean, think about it. In this day and age, that's practically a superpower.

jokes.text: So there you go! You got courage, you got integrity, you got courage again, and not litigious.
jokes.setup: Rachel listing her qualifications
jokes.punchline: Repeats courage, adds bizarre 'not litigious' qualification
jokes.explanation: Great escalating ramble. 'Courage again' shows her desperation, and 'not litigious' is hilariously specific and wrong-headed as selling point.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: I have courage, and I'm not litigious.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: I have courage, and I'm not litigious.

jokes.text: I did not mean to touch that! I mean, you! There!
jokes.setup: Rachel accidentally grabs employer inappropriately during handshake
jokes.punchline: Her flustered attempts to clarify what she touched
jokes.explanation: Physical comedy escalation. Rachel's verbal scrambling makes the situation worse - 'that' vs 'you' implies body parts vs person.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Oh my God, I am so sorry! I didn't mean to—I was just reaching for your hand and I—

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: No, it was your arm, I grabbed your arm, not—I mean, I was aiming for your hand!

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Your hand! I was going for a handshake. A normal, professional handshake.

jokes.text: Honey? The tortilla chips know what you mean.
jokes.setup: Monica being overly explicit about their sexual activities
jokes.punchline: Chandler pointing out her volume/obviousness affects inanimate objects
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation joke. The specificity of tortilla chips as witnesses to Monica's loud innuendos is absurd and memorable. Great Chandler delivery.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Oh, that's right! Yes, yes, yes!

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Could you BE any louder? The lamp is blushing.

jokes.text: Officer Bing's got a 10-100. That's pee-pee.
jokes.setup: Gary using official police codes
jokes.punchline: Translates serious-sounding code to childish bathroom term
jokes.explanation: Good juxtaposition between official police terminology and juvenile bathroom language. The contrast creates the humor.

dialogue.speaker: Gary
dialogue.line: Using official police codes...

dialogue.speaker: Gary
dialogue.line: Translates serious-sounding code to childish bathroom term

jokes.text: We violated section 12, paragraph 7 of the criminal code.
jokes.setup: Phoebe mentioning they had sex in the park
jokes.punchline: Gary's official legal terminology for public indecency
jokes.explanation: Perfect character integration - cop boyfriend frames their romantic moment in legal terms. Shows how his profession affects his language.

dialogue.speaker: Gary
dialogue.line: Well, technically it's called 'lewd conduct in a public place.'

jokes.text: But see, I've always found the men's bathroom very sexual. Haven't you?
jokes.setup: Monica following Chandler into men's room to continue competition
jokes.punchline: Trying to make bathroom seem romantic/sexual
jokes.explanation: Excellent cringe comedy. Monica's desperation to seem sexy leads to absurd bathroom romanticism. Her competitive nature overrides all logic.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Chandler? Chandler, I know you're in here.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Monica, this is the men's room. You can't be in here.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Oh please, we're past that now. I'm not leaving until I win this.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: What are you doing?

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: I'm just trying to set a little mood here. You know, some ambiance.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Monica, you're in a men's bathroom.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Exactly. And look how nice I've made it.

jokes.text: And if I did, I don't think we'd be going out.
jokes.setup: Monica asking if he finds men's bathrooms sexual
jokes.punchline: Chandler's logical observation about what that would mean for their relationship
jokes.explanation: Perfect Chandler logic cutting through Monica's absurdity. His matter-of-fact observation about the implications is both logical and hilarious.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Well, if I found men's bathrooms sexual, that would mean I'd be cheating on you every time I went to the bathroom.

jokes.text: I am the relationship king, and you are the crazy, irrational screwup!
jokes.setup: Monica having relationship anxiety for once
jokes.punchline: Chandler celebrating their role reversal with enthusiasm
jokes.explanation: Excellent role reversal payoff. Chandler's joy at finally being the stable one in the relationship is perfectly in character and quotable.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: I don't know, Chandler. What if this doesn't work out? What if we're making a huge mistake?

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Oh my God, this is the best day of my life! Finally, I get to be the confident one in this relationship!

jokes.text: By the way, thank you for loaning us Pamela and Yasmine. Man, they cannot get enough!
jokes.setup: Joey thanking friends, mentioning women's names
jokes.punchline: Reveals he's been using mannequins/inflatable dolls as the 'hot girls'
jokes.explanation: Brilliant revelation that recontextualizes entire episode. Joey's elaborate deception using mannequins is perfectly in character and creates great visual comedy.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Hey, thanks for coming guys. I want you to meet some hot girls I've been hanging out with.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Where are they?

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Oh, they're in the bedroom. Let me get them.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Guys, I'd like you to meet Bambi, Tiffany, and Crystal.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Joey... these are mannequins.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: So? They're great listeners, they never complain, and they think I'm hilarious.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: One of them is a blow-up doll.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Yeah, she's shy.

tmdb_overview: Monica becomes jealous of Phoebe and Gary. Rachel has an awkward encounter when she interviews for a new job. Joey tries to meet a girl living in Ross's building, but has trouble with finding her apartment.
insight: Mid-tier episode coasts on character dynamics despite subpar 71.6 score and sparse standout moments.
